item.title: News
item.description: Stay up to date on new cybersecurity solutions, as well as BI.ZONE news and events

item.title: We uncover vulnerability chain in Oracle VirtualBox
item.description: CVE-2025-62592 and CVE-2025-61760 rated 6.0 and 7.5 (CVSS 3.1), accordingly

item.title: Espionage clusters disguise themselves as Kyrgyz state officials
item.description: In some cases the adversaries compromise real official emails to use them in attacks on companies

item.title: BI.ZONE EDR 1.38 boasts AI assistant
item.description: New capabilities include vulnerability detection via the Threat Prediction module, operational technology protection via the ICS/OT module, and Linux self-protection

item.title: Paper Werewolf cyber spies exploit WinRAR vulnerabilities
item.description: WinRAR archiver is used by nearly 80% of Russian companies and virtually all employees whose corporate devices run on Windows

item.title: The magical comeback: new PipeMagic activity in Saudi Arabia and Brazil
item.description: Kaspersky and BI.ZONE experts have investigated the PipeMagic backdoor. Kaspersky identified changes in the operators' tactics. BI.ZONE conducted a technical analysis of the vulnerability exploited in the wild

item.title: Hacktivist attacks surge as motives grow murkier
item.description: Hacktivist clusters are behind one in five cyberattacks on Russian companies. Financial motives are becoming more prominent, and adversaries are actively adopting new tools and techniques

item.title: Fake CAPTCHAs used in new attacks on Russian companies
item.description: Close to 30 Russian companies were attacked in May and early June. The attacks utilized the ClickFix technique which tricked users into running malicious scripts. Previously, such attacks had only been recorded outside Russia

item.title: Thousand-faced spy: Silent Werewolf evolves its arsenal to stay undetected
item.description: This spring, the cluster leveraged new versions of its malware. The threat actor also introduced another tactic to obstruct incident reconstruction: instead of retrieving a malicious payload, it loaded a language model file. So far, Silent Werewolf's recent campaigns were limited to a few dozen organizations

item.title: BI.ZONE discovers two high‑impact vulnerabilities in Vaultwarden
item.description: Both have been assigned CVE identifiers

item.title: BI.ZONE Threat Intelligence upgraded to enable attack prediction and mitigate data breach impacts
item.description: The Underground resources tool is now available on the Threat Intelligence portal

item.title: Half of all threat actors targeting Russia’s energy sector are cyber spies
item.description: Sapphire Werewolf is the second espionage cluster in the last few months to have disguised phishing attempts as recruiter emails. This time, the adversaries focused on energy companies

item.title: BI.ZONE EDR 1.37 streamlines telemetry storage
item.description: The new capability simplifies and speeds up querying. Also, the macOS agent is now compatible with the Podman container engine and has new telemetry and enhanced autonomous response capabilities

item.title: Never play squid game with werewolves
item.description: Squid Werewolf emails attractive job offers

item.title: BI.ZONE EDR updated for better threat detection
item.description: The new BI.ZONE EDR version enhances the detection of complex threats for Windows. The Linux agent now monitors operations with inventory objects and is compatible with the Podman containerized environments. The users of BI.ZONE EDR can also set up task schedules to automate their routine processes
